description: Polish cycle — 3 small fixes plus smoke validation. No new feature surface, no UI work, no admin endpoint. Scope: (1) gc.Worker wedges on persistent Data.Delete failure — lift the ENOENT classifier into the data.Backend layer (wrap native ENOENT into data.ErrChunkNotFound sentinel) and ack the GC entry so a sibling-leader-swept chunk doesn't loop forever. (2) TestThreeReplicaDistribution flakes ~30% under -count=20 because seedExpiringBucket calls uuid.New() — deterministic UUID derivation by bucket index so the per-replica split is reproducible. (3) Lifecycle worker abandons partial batch on transient failure — add bounded retry (3 attempts, 1s→3s→10s backoff) for transient errors before deferring to the next tick. (4) Smoke validation + ROADMAP close-flip × 3 (2 existing latent-bug entries + 1 new-and-closed lifecycle retry per CLAUDE.md `Discovering a new gap` rule) + PRD removal. 4 stories.

userStories.title: gc.Worker ack's terminal ENOENT instead of looping
userStories.description: As an operator, I want gc.Worker to recognise that a chunk already deleted by a sibling leader is the terminal state and ack the entry so the queue keeps moving, instead of accumulating failed-but-still-listed entries in the GC backlog forever.
acceptanceCriteria: **New backend-agnostic sentinel** `data.ErrChunkNotFound` declared in internal/data/errors.go (or internal/data/data.go, wherever existing data.Err* sentinels live — verify): `var ErrChunkNotFound = errors.New("chunk not found")`. Goes in a build-tag-free file so callers compile regardless of -tags ceph.

**RADOS backend lifts** native goceph.ErrNotFound at the boundary: in internal/data/rados/backend.go `Delete` method, when `w.ioctx.Delete(oid)` returns an error matching `errors.Is(err, goceph.ErrNotFound)`, wrap and return `fmt.Errorf("chunk %s: %w", oid, data.ErrChunkNotFound)` so callers can match via `errors.Is(err, data.ErrChunkNotFound)`.

**S3 backend lifts** similarly: in internal/data/s3/backend.go `Delete` method, when the AWS SDK returns NoSuchKey, wrap and return `data.ErrChunkNotFound` so the gc worker sees the same sentinel regardless of which backend deleted the chunk.
**Memory backend** behaviour preserved — returns nil on missing chunk today (no-op delete); no change needed.

**gc.Worker fix point**: internal/gc/worker.go inner `eg.Go` body inside `drainCount` (~lines 222-228 — the `if err := w.Data.Delete(delCtx, manifest); err != nil` branch). On `errors.Is(err, data.ErrChunkNotFound)`, treat as terminal: skip the `recordErr(err)` + `subErr = err` lines, continue straight to `w.Meta.AckGCEntry(delCtx, w.Region, e)` (the same ack call used on the success path). Counter `strata_gc_terminal_ack_total{reason="enoent"}` increments before the ack.

**Non-ENOENT errors keep current behaviour**: log warn, set `subErr`, call `recordErr(err)`, return nil from inner func WITHOUT ack — next tick retries. Only `data.ErrChunkNotFound` is reclassified as terminal in this story; pool-not-found, mis-routed-cluster, transport errors stay loop-fail with warn per scope decision (operator should still investigate those).
**No new env knob** — STRATA_GC_ACK_ON_ERROR discussed in scoping and rejected (keep behaviour matrix narrow).
**Counter registration**: strata_gc_terminal_ack_total registered in internal/metrics/gc.go (or whichever file declares strata_gc_* counters — verify via grep) with `reason` label.

**Unit test** in internal/gc/worker_test.go: fake `Data` returns data.ErrChunkNotFound (wrapped) for OID X, generic `errors.New("transport fail")` for OID Y, success for OID Z. After one `RunOnce`: X is ack'd + counter incremented; Y is NOT ack'd + counter NOT bumped + stickyErr surfaces Y's error; Z is ack'd. Two more `RunOnce` calls: X never re-appears (already ack'd via gc_entries_v2 row removed); Y still re-appears (no ack); Z is gone.

**Backend Delete unit tests** confirm the lift: internal/data/rados/backend_test.go (build-tag ceph) — Delete on a non-existent OID returns an error satisfying errors.Is(err, data.ErrChunkNotFound). Skip the test under default tag (`-tags ceph` required). internal/data/s3/backend_test.go — same shape with mocked NoSuchKey AWS response.
`go vet ./...` and `go vet -tags ceph ./...` both pass
`go test -race ./internal/gc/... ./internal/data/...` passes
Typecheck passes
Tests pass

userStories.title: Deterministic seed for TestThreeReplicaDistribution
userStories.description: As a developer, I want `make test -race -count=N` to be reliable signal for the changes I'm working on, not flake ~30% of the time on an unrelated distribution test.
acceptanceCriteria: **Root cause** is internal/lifecycle/distribute_test.go:54 — `seedExpiringBucket` generates the bucket UUID via `uuid.New().String()`. With 9 random UUIDs + `bucketReplicaIndex` (FNV-32a mod 3), the per-replica counts legitimately drift to 6/3/0 or 7/2/0 — the test asserts the split lands inside [1, 5] per replica and fails ~30% of runs.

**Fix shape A — preferred**: add a `seedExpiringBucketWithID(t *testing.T, ctx, store, be, name string, id uuid.UUID)` variant that accepts a caller-supplied UUID. `seedExpiringBucket` becomes a thin wrapper calling the new variant with `uuid.New()` so the other 4 call sites (lines 76, 204, 243, and the existing line 120 if not migrated) keep their current shape.

**TestThreeReplicaDistribution** (the test starting at the function definition near line 111) switches to the new variant: derive each bucket UUID via `uuid.NewSHA1(uuid.Nil, []byte(fmt.Sprintf("bucket-%d", i)))` so the FNV-32a-mod-3 hash is reproducible run-to-run.

**Hard-coded expected split**: compute the deterministic per-replica counts once (run the test, observe the triple e.g. [3]int{3, 3, 3} or whatever falls out) and replace the `[1, 5] guard` assertion with the exact triple via `require.Equal(t, [3]int{a, b, c}, counts)`.
**Invariant preserved**: the `totalDeletes == buckets` assertion (the real correctness signal — every bucket processed exactly once across replicas) stays exactly as it is today.
Run `go test -race -count=20 -run TestThreeReplicaDistribution ./internal/lifecycle/...` → 20/20 pass (today: ~14/20).

**Touch only TestThreeReplicaDistribution + the new helper.** Do NOT modify the other 4 callers of `seedExpiringBucket` — keeping them on random UUIDs preserves their existing test coverage of non-deterministic edge cases.
`go vet ./...` passes
Typecheck passes
Tests pass
userStories.notes: Implemented: added memory.Store.CreateBucketWithID test-helper (Cassandra/TiKV don't need it — UUIDs are server-side). seedExpiringBucketWithID variant pins UUID via uuid.NewSHA1(uuid.Nil, "bucket-N"); deterministic split for 9 buckets / 3 replicas is [4,1,4]. 20/20 pass at -race -count=20. [1,5] guard replaced by hard-coded triple equality.

userStories.title: Lifecycle worker bounded transient-error retry
userStories.description: As an operator, I want the lifecycle worker to retry a transient failure (network blip, 503, ctx-deadline) before abandoning the remainder of the batch to the next tick, so a 200 ms network hiccup doesn't delay 999 successful expirations by 5 minutes.
acceptanceCriteria: **Call site**: internal/lifecycle/worker.go per-action calls — `expireNoncurrent` line 393 (`w.Meta.DeleteObject(ctx, b.ID, v.Key, v.VersionID, true)`), plus any other per-object `Data.Delete` / `Meta.DeleteObject` site inside `applyRule` / `applyNoncurrentActions` (grep `w.Meta.DeleteObject\|w.Data.Delete` in the file to enumerate). Wrap each in a bounded retry: 3 attempts total, backoff `1s → 3s → 10s` (sleep BEFORE the 2nd + 3rd attempt).
**Hard-coded retry budget**; no STRATA_LIFECYCLE_RETRY_* env knob (configurable variant rejected in scoping — narrow this cycle).

**Transient classifier** internal/lifecycle/retry.go (or fold into worker.go if < 30 LoC): `isTransient(err) bool` returns true for `errors.Is(err, context.DeadlineExceeded)`, `errors.Is(err, syscall.ECONNRESET)`, `errors.Is(err, syscall.ETIMEDOUT)`, and any error whose chain contains an `interface{ Temporary() bool }` returning true. Returns false for `errors.Is(err, meta.ErrNotFound)`, `errors.Is(err, data.ErrChunkNotFound)`, `errors.Is(err, data.ErrNoSuchKey)`, `errors.Is(err, data.ErrNoSuchBucket)`, and any error not satisfying the transient predicate.
**Terminal errors short-circuit** the retry loop (no sleep, no second attempt) — caller continues to the next action immediately.
**Ctx-aware sleep**: between attempts, race `time.After(d)` against `ctx.Done()` so a cancelled lifecycle worker exits its retry loop immediately on shutdown — return `ctx.Err()` if cancelled.

**Counter** strata_lifecycle_retry_total{outcome="ok|exhausted|terminal"} stamps each call: `ok` = succeeded on attempt > 1 (no bump if first attempt succeeds), `exhausted` = 3 attempts all transient-failed, `terminal` = first attempt hit a terminal error. Registered in internal/metrics/lifecycle.go.

**Helper** `retryAction(ctx context.Context, fn func() error) error` is the single retry entry point so call sites stay compact: `if err := retryAction(ctx, func() error { return w.Meta.DeleteObject(...) }); err != nil { ... }`.

**Unit test** internal/lifecycle/retry_test.go: table-driven — (a) success on first attempt → no retry, no counter bump; (b) transient × 2 then success → 2 sleeps total (use injectable `sleep func(time.Duration)` for test determinism), counter `ok=1`; (c) transient × 3 → counter `exhausted=1`, returns last error; (d) terminal on first attempt → no retry, counter `terminal=1`; (e) ctx-cancel mid-backoff → retry loop returns immediately with ctx.Err().
`go vet ./...` passes
`go test -race ./internal/lifecycle/...` passes
Typecheck passes
Tests pass
userStories.notes: Implemented: internal/lifecycle/retry.go with retryAction (production seam) + retryActionWith (test seam taking injectable sleeper). 3 attempts, backoffs [1s, 3s] consumed before attempts 2+3. isTransient classifies context.DeadlineExceeded / syscall.ECONNRESET / syscall.ETIMEDOUT / errors satisfying interface{Temporary()bool}; meta.ErrObjectNotFound / meta.ErrBucketNotFound / data.ErrChunkNotFound / data.ErrNotFound short-circuit as terminal. ctxSleep races time.After against ctx.Done so shutdown drops out instantly. Wired into expire + expireNoncurrent in worker.go (the two Meta.DeleteObject sites — no Data.Delete per-action sites in the lifecycle worker today; transition's GetChunks/PutChunks/SetObjectStorage chain stays unretried per scope). Counter strata_lifecycle_retry_total{outcome=ok|terminal|exhausted} registered. PRD spec listed '1s→3s→10s' but AC and tests only consume 2 sleeps; retryBackoffs keeps the 10s slot for a future 4th-attempt extension without rewriting tests.

userStories.title: Smoke validation + ROADMAP close-flip + PRD removal
userStories.description: As a future-maintainer, I want one explicit verification pass that proves all three fixes landed correctly, plus the ROADMAP entries flipped and the PRD markdown removed per the cycle-end-to-end discipline.
acceptanceCriteria: Run `go test -race -count=5 -run TestThreeReplicaDistribution ./internal/lifecycle/...` → 5/5 pass (proves US-002 flake fix is stable across multiple runs).
Run full `go test -race ./...` (default tag) → green; capture duration in progress.txt.
Run `go test -tags ceph ./internal/data/rados/...` if Docker available (build-tag ceph backend unit tests verify ENOENT lift); else document the expected pass in progress.txt.
Run `make vet` → green; run `make vet` equivalent with `-tags ceph` if convenient.
Run `go build ./...` + `go build -tags ceph ./...` both succeed.

ROADMAP.md close-flip in the same commit: (a) `gc.Worker.drainCount infinite-loops when Data.Delete fails persistently` (latent-bug entry under Known latent bugs) → flipped to Done, summary references US-001, the data.ErrChunkNotFound sentinel lift in the backend Delete impls, and the new strata_gc_terminal_ack_total{reason="enoent"} counter. (b) `TestThreeReplicaDistribution is flaky` (latent-bug entry) → flipped to Done, summary references US-002 deterministic-PRNG-via-uuid.NewSHA1 approach + the hard-coded expected split.

**NEW P3 entry** for `Lifecycle worker no-retry on transient failures` added under `Correctness & consistency` section AND closed in same commit (per CLAUDE.md `Discovering a new gap` rule — US-003 is not pre-roadmapped). Summary references the 3-attempt 1s→3s→10s retry, ctx-aware sleep racing time.After against ctx.Done, strata_lifecycle_retry_total counter, and the transient/terminal classifier shape.

Each close-flip carries `(commit <pending>)` placeholder per the established cycle convention; the SHA backfill lands on main post-merge as the fast-follow commit (same pattern as the prior cycle's f6a5476 → backfill commit).

tasks/prd-polish-dx.md REMOVED via `git rm` per CLAUDE.md PRD lifecycle rule (markdown PRD is disposable; canonical record is the Ralph auto-archive pair under scripts/ralph/archive/<date>-<branch>/).
scripts/ralph/progress.txt carries one US-004 block summarising smoke results + any learnings for future iterations.
`go vet ./...` passes
All tests pass
Typecheck passes
Tests pass
